theme: Every silent element in the office is a story waiting to be told — the dark monitor, the forgotten deep-work focus, the moment the whole team clicks in
visual_observations: Deep scan of all four source files. The `char--deep-work` class is assigned in Office.jsx after 45 min of working but has zero CSS — a fully silent, invisible class. The `.desk__monitor` is a static dark box with no hover life. The `data-has-error` flag never reaches the floor wrapper, so error state only screams at desk level while the room stays calm. The `onWorkerClick` callback fires but leaves the office floor visually dumb — no floor-side echo. WallClock and whiteboard are sharp. Conference table reacts to vibe beautifully. The `vibeStreak`-driven `plantStage` is computed but nothing renders it — dead code on the floor. Coffee corner chat badge is a nice ambient touch. All 7 review-#36 ideas (mobile fill, window vibe tint, wander fan-out, away sign escalation, clock chime, cactus barometer, phantom) remain unimplemented and carry forward.

ideas.title: Deep Work Focus Halo — Give the Silent Class a Voice
ideas.description: In Office.jsx line ~380, `char--deep-work` is already added to working characters whose `updated_at` is >45 min old — but there is ZERO CSS for it. It is a completely invisible class. Fix in Office.css: `.char--deep-work .char__avatar { border-color: #6366f1 !important; animation: deep-focus-halo 3s ease-in-out infinite !important; }` and `@keyframes deep-focus-halo { 0%,100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(99,102,241,0), 0 3px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); } 50% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 6px rgba(99,102,241,0.45), 0 0 18px rgba(99,102,241,0.2), 0 3px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); } }`. Also add `.char--deep-work .char__name::after { content: ' 🎧'; }` so the name label quietly appends the headphones emoji. WHY: A class being applied for 45 minutes of focus with zero user-visible effect is a waste of the system's own knowledge. This is a 6-line CSS fix that transforms invisible data into readable ambient narrative.

ideas.title: Desk Monitor Cursor Blink — The Dark Box Gets a Heartbeat
ideas.description: Every `.desk__monitor` is a static dark rectangle with no content or interactivity. Add a blinking cursor pseudo-element that activates on desk hover, making it feel like a live terminal. In Office.css: `.desk:hover .desk__monitor:not(.desk__monitor--vacant) { position: relative; }` `.desk:hover .desk__monitor:not(.desk__monitor--vacant)::before { content: '▋'; position: absolute; bottom: 2px; left: 3px; font-size: 5px; line-height: 1; color: var(--role-color, #a78bfa); opacity: 0.75; animation: cursor-blink 0.9s step-start infinite; }` `@keyframes cursor-blink { 0%,100%{opacity:0.75} 50%{opacity:0} }`. For active desks, override monitor hover glow: `.desk--active:hover .desk__monitor { box-shadow: 0 0 16px color-mix(in srgb, var(--role-color, var(--accent)) 80%, transparent) !important; background: #0a0a1e !important; animation: none !important; }`. WHY: The monitor is the most recognizable desk object — making it respond to hover with even a single pixel of life makes the whole floor feel interactive rather than decorative.

ideas.title: Error Room Tint — The Floor Bleeds Red When Something Is Wrong
ideas.description: Currently when a worker has `status: 'error'`, only the individual desk gets `desk--error` tint and the character gets the red halo. The office floor itself stays visually calm. Fix: In the Office.jsx office-floor div opening tag, add `data-has-error={workers.some(w => w.status === 'error') ? '' : undefined}`. In Office.css add: `.office-floor[data-has-error]::before { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 55% at center, transparent 25%, rgba(239,68,68,0.07) 100%); pointer-events: none; z-index: 0; animation: error-room-throb 1.8s ease-in-out infinite; border-radius: 14px; }` `@keyframes error-room-throb { 0%,100%{opacity:0.55} 50%{opacity:1} }`. Also tint the floor border: `.office-floor[data-has-error] { border-color: rgba(239,68,68,0.35) !important; }`. WHY: A glance at the office should tell you the health of the team. Right now errors are invisible unless you're looking at the right desk.

ideas.title: Character Click Sonar Ping — The Floor Echoes the Worker Card Scroll
ideas.description: The `onWorkerClick` callback already fires when a character is clicked in Office.jsx, but the office floor gives zero visual feedback — the worker card scrolls into view off-screen and the character just sits there. Add a `pingedId` state to the Office component: `const [pingedId, setPingedId] = useState(null)`. Wrap the existing `onWorkerClick` call inside Character: `onClick={() => { onWorkerClick?.(worker); setPingedId(worker.id); setTimeout(() => setPingedId(null), 700); }}`. Pass `isPinged={pingedId === worker.id}` and add `char--pinged` class when true. In Office.css: `.char--pinged .char__avatar::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: -3px; border-radius: 50%; border: 2px solid var(--role-color, var(--accent)); animation: sonar-ping 0.7s ease-out forwards; pointer-events: none; }` `@keyframes sonar-ping { from { opacity: 0.9; transform: scale(0.85); } to { opacity: 0; transform: scale(2.4); } }`. WHY: Without this, clicking a tiny avatar and having it do nothing visible is a UI mystery. The sonar ring confirms the tap in 0.7 seconds and looks cool doing it.

ideas.title: Vibe Forecast Weather Widget — One Glyph Reads the Room
ideas.description:
The vibe is conveyed through the pill header, the whiteboard, the monitor glow, the window — but never as a single scannable ambient glyph on the floor surface itself. Add a tiny weather widget below the coffee corner. In Office.jsx JSX, after the coffee-corner div: `
{VIBE_WEATHER_ICON[vibe]}
`. Define `const VIBE_WEATHER_ICON = { 'on-fire':'⛈️', 'crushing':'☀️', 'in-flow':'🌤️', 'slow-day':'🌧️', 'after-hours':'🌙' }`. CSS: `.vibe-weather { position: absolute; right: 2.5%; top: 63%; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1; pointer-events: none; z-index: 1; opacity: 0.6; transition: opacity 0.4s; }` `.vibe-weather--on-fire { animation: weather-urgent 1.2s ease-in-out infinite; }` `@keyframes weather-urgent { 0%,100%{transform:scale(1)} 50%{transform:scale(1.15)} }`. On vibe change the existing key-prop re-renders will trigger the transition naturally. WHY: A weather widget is a universal language — you glance at the office and know instantly. It stacks with the rest of the vibe system without duplicating it.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Chalk-Erase Transition — The Vibe Change Has a Gesture
ideas.description: When vibeKey changes, the whiteboard text swaps instantly because `key={vibe}` triggers a React remount but the span has no transition animation. The class `wb-vibe-text` already exists and `key={vibe}` is on the nameplate. Apply the key trick to the text span itself: add `key={vibe}` to the `<span className='wb-vibe-text'>` element. Then in Office.css replace or augment the existing `.wb-vibe-text` rule: `.wb-vibe-text { display: inline-block; animation: chalk-write 0.55s ease forwards; }` `@keyframes chalk-write { 0% { clip-path: inset(0 100% 0 0); opacity: 0.3; } 100% { clip-path: inset(0 0% 0 0); opacity: 1; } }`. For the erase side, add a `::before` to the whiteboard that briefly animates on key change: `.office-whiteboard .wb-vibe-text::before { content: ''; display: block; }`. Also add chalk dust via whiteboard `::after`: `.office-whiteboard[data-vibe]::after { content: '···'; position: absolute; right: 3px; top: 38%; font-size: 0.3rem; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.25); animation: chalk-dust 0.4s ease forwards; }` `@keyframes chalk-dust { 0%{opacity:0.6;transform:translateX(0)} 100%{opacity:0;transform:translateX(6px) translateY(-3px)} }`. WHY: Vibe is the most important state in the app and it changes with zero ceremony. A chalk-writing animation costs 4 CSS lines and makes vibe transitions feel intentional.

ideas.title: Full-Sync Aura — When Everyone Is In, the Office Glows
ideas.description:
Compute `const isFullSync = nonMgr.length > 0 && idleWorkers.length === 0 && workingWorkers.length === nonMgr.length` in the Office component. Add `data-full-sync={isFullSync || undefined}` to the `.office-floor` div. In Office.css: `.office-floor[data-full-sync] { border-color: var(--accent) !important; animation: full-sync-border 3.5s ease-in-out infinite; }` `@keyframes full-sync-border { 0%,100%{box-shadow: 0 8px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.35), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.05)} 50%{box-shadow: 0 8px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.35), 0 0 0 3px rgba(167,139,250,0.35), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.08)} }`. Also render a tiny centered banner: `{isFullSync &&
⚡ Full sync
}` positioned at `top: 0; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); position: absolute`. CSS: `.full-sync-banner { font-size: 0.45rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--accent); background: rgba(167,139,250,0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(167,139,250,0.25); border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 2px 10px; z-index: 5; white-space: nowrap; animation: banner-drop 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) both; }` `@keyframes banner-drop { from{opacity:0;transform:translateX(-50%) translateY(-8px)} to{opacity:1;transform:translateX(-50%) translateY(0)} }`. WHY: Full team sync is rare and should feel electric. The glowing floor border + the tiny banner create a team-reward moment that only appears when everyone's locked in — which makes it meaningful.
